Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

HTS Market Set to Reach $76B as Industry Enters Terabit Era

Paris, France – March, 2026 – Novaspace’s latest High Throughput Satellites (HTS) report shows global demand reaching 218 Tbps by 2034, while service revenues are set to more than double to $76 billion over the same period. The findings […]...

Airlines and aviation operators should prepare for exponential connectivity capacity growth, as HTS revenue doubling will enable advanced in-flight services and operational technologies previously constrained by satellite bandwidth limitations.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Brazil Rolls Out First Supersonic Fighter Built in-Country

A Saab-Embraer program leads to first supersonic fighter assembled in Brazil.

Brazil's domestic fighter assembly capability signals a shift toward regional aerospace self-sufficiency and reduces dependence on foreign military aircraft imports.

Dreamliner groundings push Kenya Airways back into the red

Kenya Airways returned to financial losses in 2025, the airline reported on March 24, 2026. The Kenyan flag…...

Aircraft availability disruptions from supply chain issues can rapidly erode profitability for smaller carriers dependent on specific platforms.

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

FAA Presses California for Records in Airport-Funding Dispute

California airport funding dispute draws new FAA scrutiny.

FAA's records request signals potential enforcement action regarding state airport funding compliance with federal grant obligations and airport development requirements.

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la de la primera misión tripulada Artemis alrededor de la Luna

Diversos eventos previos al lanzamiento, del lanzamiento y de la misión Artemis II de la NASA alrededor de la Luna se transmitirán en línea. La agencia tiene como fecha objetivo no antes del miércoles 1 de abril para este vuelo de prueba, con una ventana de lanzamiento de dos horas que se abre a...

NASA's April 1 target for Artemis II represents a critical validation of human deep-space capabilities required for sustainable lunar operations.

See NASA →

DJI Avata 360 Sets New Standards for Immersive 360 FPV Flying

Equipped with DJI’s flagship 1-inch imaging, O4+ video transmission, and omnidirectional obstacle sensing, creators can now...

DJI's 360-degree FPV capability with omnidirectional sensing eliminates traditional blind spots, enabling safer autonomous flight paths for commercial drone operations beyond visual line of sight.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Southwest Airlines Transforms Austin-Bergstrom Airport, Unleashing A New Era For Travelers And Crew

The carrier has scheduled almost 3,500 departures from the Texan facility this month alone.

Southwest's massive Austin deployment signals aggressive domestic capacity expansion as carriers leverage post-pandemic demand recovery to capture regional market share.

Pentagon eyes redirecting Ukraine’s Patriot missiles to Middle East

The Pentagon is considering whether to redirect air defense interceptor missiles originally earmarked for Ukraine to the Middle…...

U.S. military supply chain prioritization between theaters could accelerate defense contractor production capacity expansion to meet competing geopolitical demands.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Virgin Australia named world’s best cabin crew for eighth year running

Virgin Australia has been named World’s Best Cabin Crew for the eighth consecutive year in the AirlineRatings.com World’s…...

Sustained service excellence recognition signals that consistent crew training investments and culture directly influence customer loyalty and competitive differentiation in crowded markets.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport Remains North America's Busiest By Total Passengers

With over 100 million passengers handled in 2025, ATL continues to dominate global rankings for the world's busiest airport.

ATL's sustained 100M+ annual passenger volume demonstrates the critical infrastructure demands on major hubs and their outsized influence on North American network connectivity.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike fatalities persist because pilots systematically overestimate their reaction capacity in low-altitude operations, making wire avoidance training a critical safety imperative.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s Hubble Detects First-Ever Spin Reversal of Tiny Comet

Astronomers using NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope have found evidence that the spinning of a small comet slowed and then reversed its direction of rotation, offering a dramatic example of how volatile activity can affect the spin and physical evolution of small bodies in the solar system. This is the...

Observing comet rotation dynamics through Hubble advances understanding of how outgassing forces reshape small celestial bodies, informing predictions for asteroid deflection missions.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Hughes targets sovereign satcom demand with network control software

Company rolls out AI-enabled modem as militaries shift to hybrid networks...

Hughes' AI-enabled modem addresses military satcom operators' critical need to seamlessly integrate legacy and modern satellite systems without costly infrastructure replacement.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Video Shows Russian Geran Drone Shot Down by Rifle-Armed Ukrainian Yak-52 Crew

A first-person point-of-view video shows the crew member in the rear seat of a Ukrainian Yak-52 shooting with a rifle at a Russian Geran drone, which later detonates in a powerful explosion. A video that emerged on Mar. 24, 2026, shows a Russian Geran-type One-Way Attack (OWA) drone exploding...

Ukrainian military has adapted vintage Yak-52 trainers into armed intercept platforms, demonstrating improvised air defense alternatives when conventional systems are unavailable.

See The Aviationist →

▶
Safety Mar 26, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure at RNO requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural complexities that deviate from standard departure operations at the airport.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Mar 26, 2026

$147,000 Damage: Military KC-46A Tanker Rips Up 25 Foot Section Of Airport Tarmac

The continuous high temperature and pressure from the engine blast affected the integrity of the parking ramp, resulting in this damage.

Engine exhaust damage to airport infrastructure reveals critical need for KC-46A parking protocols and potential facility upgrades at military bases.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Here’s How Much Larger The Boeing 777-300ER’s Windows Are Compared To The Airbus A350

Did you know that the older Triple-Seven has larger windows than a 21st-century clean-sheet carbon-composite widebody?

Boeing's 777-300ER windows exceed A350 dimensions despite being two decades older, indicating that cabin comfort amenities weren't prioritized equally in Airbus's latest widebody design.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

AirAsia Philippines risks losing licenses over $14.5 million unpaid fees

The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) has issued a final demand letter to AirAsia Philippines for…...

Regulatory enforcement actions on unpaid fees signal heightened compliance scrutiny that could force operational disruptions at major carriers across emerging markets.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational efficiency gains across airline, airport, and flight operations offer immediate sustainability improvements without waiting for next-generation aircraft technology maturation.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Volotea closes smaller than anticipated funding round following strong results

Regional low-cost carrier Volotea has completed its latest capital increase round. The airline announced in September 2024 that…...

Volotea's smaller-than-expected funding round despite operational strength may signal investor caution toward European regional carriers or shift toward profitability-driven growth over expansion.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Toy Soldiers Bricking It: Danish Mix-Up Nearly Disassembles LegoLand Plane.

Back in September 2025, during a period of intense, unexplained unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) activity,...

Supply chain disruptions from unexpected UAV interference can compromise aviation operations even at leisure facilities, highlighting critical vulnerabilities in ground logistics during emerging airspace threats.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The Airline That Introduced Lie-Flat Business Class Seats To The World

Find out everything you need to know about the airlines that introduced lie-flat business class seats to the world.

The innovation of lie-flat seats fundamentally transformed business class economics by justifying premium pricing and creating a competitive differentiation that now defines long-haul carrier strategies.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Golden Dome and the velocity race: Why ground-based optics are the key to mission persistence

While often described as a moonshot for missile defense, the Golden Dome for America mission is in reality something even more challenging: an exercise in disciplined systems engineering under threat. Hypersonic glide vehicles, maneuvering ballistic missiles and complex decoys are compressing...

Ground-based optical tracking systems are becoming critical infrastructure for detecting advanced hypersonic threats, requiring aerospace manufacturers to prioritize sensor integration over raw speed capabilities.

See SpaceNews →

Business Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Bombardier delivers first Global 8000 to NetJets

Bombardier has handed over the first Global 8000 business jet to NetJets, marking the fleet launch customer’s entry…...

NetJets' adoption of the Global 8000 signals fractional ownership's embrace of ultra-long-range capability, potentially reshaping executive travel patterns and market demand for high-end cabin technology.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Up To 17-Hour Nonstop: Emirates' 10 Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Emirates is the world's second-largest long-haul operator, after United Airlines. In 2026, the carrier has an average of 134 daily long-haul passenger departures from its Dubai hub. Over half of the carrier's total flights fall into this category.

Emirates' expansion to 17-hour routes demonstrates growing ultra-long-haul viability, with long-haul flights representing over 50% of their operation—signaling hub-and-spoke strategy resilience against point-to-point competitors.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

United Airlines transforms devil’s chariot into premium CRJ-450

LOS ANGELES — Before the COVID-19 pandemic upended the airline industry, one-third of all United Airlines’ domestic US departures were on 50-seat regional jets. While that number has fallen to just 10% today, United isn’t resting on its laurels as demand for premium travel products remains high....

United's regional jet redeployment strategy prioritizes premium cabin retrofits over capacity growth, capitalizing on sustained demand for higher-margin business travel despite lower regional fleet utilization.

See Runway Girl Network →

Military Mar 26, 2026

South Korea rolls out first mass-production KF-21 Boramae

South Korea has rolled out the first mass-produced KF-21 Boramae fighter jet, completing a major production milestone for…...

South Korea's KF-21 mass production demonstrates indigenous fighter development capability, reducing dependence on foreign aircraft and strengthening regional defense industrial autonomy.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI Avata 360 global launch begins, US delayed again

DJI has kicked off the global rollout of the Avata 360, positioning it as its most immersive consumer drone yet. In many parts of the world, that means orders are either already live or pre-sales have begun. In the US, though, things are playing out a little differently, again. more…...

DJI's inability to secure US market access for its latest consumer drone model underscores the ongoing regulatory barriers Chinese manufacturers face in American markets.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Up To 16-Hour Nonstop Flights: Etihad Airways' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Two of the routes are brand-new this year, while another started in 2025. Learn more here!

Etihad's expansion of ultra-long routes signals growing demand for nonstop connectivity to remote markets, requiring aircraft with extended range capabilities and operational efficiency.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Changi Airports International to help develop Phu Quoc as ‘airport destination’

Changi Airports International (CAI) announced it has signed a strategic cooperation agreement with Vietnam’s Sun Group to develop…...

Singapore's airport operator is expanding regional influence by partnering to transform a Vietnamese resort island into an integrated aviation and tourism hub, signaling consolidation of Southeast Asian gateway development.

Military Mar 26, 2026

First Production KF-21 Boramae Rolled Out of Factory in South Korea

The first serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jet has rolled out of the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory in a major milestone for the program. The first of 40 serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jets has rolled out of the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory, marking a major...

South Korea's indigenous fighter jet program transitions from development to serial production, establishing domestic combat aircraft manufacturing capability and reducing reliance on foreign defense suppliers.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to test nuclear electric propulsion with 2028 mission to Mars

NASA’s new exploration strategy includes the rapid development of a nuclear-powered mission to Mars in 2028, leveraging hardware originally built for the lunar Gateway.

Repurposing existing lunar Gateway hardware for nuclear propulsion accelerates Mars mission timelines while reducing development costs and technical risk.

See SpaceNews →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Airbus tests firefighting system linking drones, aircraft and ground crews

Airbus has demonstrated a concept in which diverse aerial and ground assets share a common data picture during…...

Integrated multi-asset coordination during emergency response could significantly reduce response times and improve situational awareness across traditionally siloed aviation operations.
